expectations.text: References at least one script by filename
expectations.evidence: References 'python3 scripts/pipeline_analyzer.py --platform github --workflow .github/workflows/ci.yml' in Part 1 and 'python3 scripts/ci_health.py --platform github --repo your-org/your-repo --limit 20' in Part 10.

Includes numerous runnable commands: 'npm ci', 'npx turbo run build --filter=[HEAD^1]', 'npx playwright test --shard=...', 'npx jest --shard=...', 'docker push ghcr.io/...', and complete GitHub Actions workflow YAML with inline commands.

expectations.text: Links to a relevant reference file
expectations.evidence: References the skill's design philosophy ('Fail Fast -> Parallelize -> Cache -> Secure -> Deploy') and DevSecOps best practices but does not explicitly cite references/best_practices.md, references/security.md, references/optimization.md, or references/devsecops.md by filename.
Follows the CI/CD skill's core workflow: 'Fail Fast -> Parallelize -> Cache -> Secure -> Deploy', with 10 structured parts covering diagnosis, optimization, security, and deployment.
Organized as 10 numbered parts with a 4-week implementation plan (Foundation -> Test Optimization -> Security Integration -> Deployment & Hardening), plus tables comparing before/after timing.
